title: HLA-C miR-148a canonical 8-mer site is absent in 0/3,284 alleles, but the 7-mer seed is present in ~25% — partially dissolved by length audit

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'HLA-C miR-148a canonical 8-mer site is absent in 0/3,284 alleles, but the 7-mer '
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

operations.note: Canonical mir148a binding: the full 8-mer 'AGCACTGA' (seed + 1 anchor base). Pre-registered as the binding-competent motif.
First NotExpr in the corpus: complement of the canonical-positive set.

First OrExpr in the corpus: the partition for the Phase 6C length-audit reframe — 'canonical OR seed-only'. The Phase 6C audit found that the 7-mer seed appears in ~25% of long-annotation alleles, partially dissolving the absence-of-canonical-binding framing.

statistics.name: n_alleles_with_canonical_mir148a_site
statistics.note: Zero of 3,284 HLA-C alleles carry the canonical 8-mer.

statistics.name: n_alleles_with_seed_or_canonical_mir148a
statistics.note: Approximately 25% of long-annotation alleles carry the 7-mer seed even though none carry the canonical 8-mer. Phase 6C finding.

statistics.name: fraction_with_seed_or_canonical
statistics.note: Derived: s_n_seed_or_canonical / 3284 ~ 0.25. The seed-only presence partially dissolves the original 'HLA-C escapes mir148a regulation' framing.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts encode the qualified-positive register: (1) the canonical 8-mer is strictly absent (n=0) — the original observation holds; (2) the 7-mer seed is present at non-trivial frequency (>5%) — the Phase 6C length audit dissolves the absolute 'no mir148a binding capacity' interpretation. Outcome qualified_positive: the absence of the canonical motif is real, but the broader 'HLA-C is mir148a-immune' framing is wrong because seed-only sites can still bind weakly.
inference.failure_mode: If s_n_canonical > 0, the absolute absence claim fails. If s_seed_fraction <= 0.05, the dissolution would not have triggered and the original framing would not need qualification.

conclusion.assertion: Across 3,284 HLA-C 4-field alleles, zero carry the canonical mir-148a 8-mer binding site (AGCACTGA), but ~25% carry the 7-mer seed (AGCACTG) on long-annotation 3'UTR sequences. The original 'HLA-C escapes mir-148a regulation via canonical-site absence' framing is partially dissolved by the Phase 6C length audit: canonical-site absence is real, but seed-only sites can still confer weak binding and the broader regulatory-immunity interpretation is not licensed.

scope.scale_note: HLA-C 3'UTR specifically; HLA-A and HLA-B 3'UTR mir148a status are sibling claims (HLA-A: known canonical binding; HLA-B: variable).

confidence.note: Census-style claim; confidence reflects manifest completeness.

external_assumptions.statement: miR-148a 7-mer seed binding is biologically meaningful even without the canonical 8-mer anchor base; seed-only matches typically confer weaker but non-zero regulation.
The Phase 6C length audit correctly identified that short 3'UTR annotations in IMGT could mask the seed-only motif; the seed-or-canonical count is computed only over long-annotation alleles.
